scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a standard protection claim only when receipt, contract, serial, diagnostic, deductible, and service-path evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing contract, purchase, serial, and service-history prerequisites produced a blocked-state exception note instead of improvised execution.
The lane refused closure without repair or replacement confirmation, customer notice, and financial or deductible evidence.
Cancellation and tender mismatches stayed traceable and were routed into the correct merchant-side correction or billing-dispute path rather than resolved by guesswork.
Unsupported coverage exceptions were treated as approval-bound policy cases instead of routine claim execution.
Recalled products triggered stop-processing and safety escalation instead of ordinary protection-plan repair or replacement handling.
High-volume queue handling preserved lifecycle-state discipline and did not skip contract, deductible, or diagnostic gates.
Serial mismatch, conflicting damage history, and repeated-claim signals caused a hold-and-escalate response to loss prevention rather than unauthorized completion.
The lane preserved protection-plan terminology such as ADH, BER, service fee, dispatch, depot repair, and free-look cancellation instead of reverting to generic support language.
Pricing, deductible-waiver, and goodwill-credit authority requests were rejected cleanly and handed off to the correct adjacent owner.
Missing tenant jurisdiction, carrier, service-network, and contract-version context triggered low-confidence escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Conflicting public, tenant, and manufacturer or recall policy signals were recorded as a precedence conflict that required escalation.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside product-protection execution and does not claim pricing override, legal, insurer-adjudication, fraud-adjudication, or safety-signoff authority.
